stage.name: Morning Commute
stage.objective: Explain ordinary inference and the representation steps that turn prompt/response text into token IDs, vectors, and tensors without implying durable weight updates.

verification.npmInstall: up to date; audited 25 packages; 0 vulnerabilities
verification.build: passed with existing Vite large-chunk warning
verification.buildPages: passed with existing Vite large-chunk warning

actualCards.title: Inference
actualCards.subtitle: A forward pass, not a memory update
actualCards.oneSentenceDefinition: Inference is normal model use: fixed learned weights process the current context to produce scores for the next response token.
actualCards.recommendation: Keep and lightly revise the visual so fixed weights and temporary activation states are unmistakable.

actualCards.title: Prompt vs Response
actualCards.subtitle: Given context versus generated tokens
actualCards.oneSentenceDefinition: Prompt tokens are given to the model; response tokens are generated by the model one at a time and appended to the context.
actualCards.checkpointCorrect: It is appended to the context
actualCards.recommendation: Keep as a distinct card from Autoregression; make response-so-far visibly part of the next context.

actualCards.title: Tokenization
actualCards.subtitle: Text becomes model-readable chunks
actualCards.oneSentenceDefinition: Tokenization splits text into chunks that can be represented as token IDs.
actualCards.checkpointCorrect: A word, word piece, punctuation mark, or other text chunk
actualCards.recommendation: Show uneven chunks, punctuation, spaces, and word pieces more explicitly.

actualCards.title: Token IDs
actualCards.subtitle: Chunks become lookup numbers
actualCards.oneSentenceDefinition: A token ID is the number assigned to a token so the model can look up its learned starting vector.
actualCards.recommendation: Separate tokenizer output, ID mapping, and embedding table in the next visual.

actualCards.title: Embeddings
actualCards.subtitle: Token IDs look up learned starting vectors
actualCards.oneSentenceDefinition: An embedding is a learned starting vector retrieved for a token ID.
actualCards.checkpointCorrect: A learned starting vector for a token ID
actualCards.recommendation: Make durable embedding table versus temporary retrieved starting vector more concrete; strongest textless Image 2 candidate.

actualCards.title: Vectors
actualCards.subtitle: Lists of numbers carrying features
actualCards.oneSentenceDefinition: A vector is a list of numbers that represents features of something, such as a token.
actualCards.checkpointCorrect: Embeddings and hidden states are both vectors
actualCards.recommendation: High-priority revision. Improve the tiny interaction and keep the distributed-feature limitation prominent.

actualCards.title: Tensors
actualCards.subtitle: Organized blocks of vectors
actualCards.oneSentenceDefinition: A tensor is an organized block of numbers, often holding many token vectors at once.
actualCards.recommendation: High-priority revision. Use lightweight CSS 3D or a coded axis inspector for token positions and feature dimensions.

highestPriorityIssues: Embeddings durable table versus temporary retrieved vector

image2Readiness.reason: Embeddings could benefit from a textless generated prop, but label-heavy diagrams should be repaired first in coded SVG/HTML.

manualQa.homeGeneratedAssets: Generated Home mark and hero loaded from assets/generated/home at 390px.
manualQa.beforeMorningGeneratedAssets: Before Morning generated asset loaded in the visual-aid review route.
manualQa.whereLlmsFitTaxonomy: AI Family Tree visual loaded in the visual-aid review route and remains coded SVG.
manualQa.bottomNav: Bottom nav remained visible; dense-card spot checks at 320px and 430px did not hide the visual aid panels.
manualQa.checkpointRandomization: Two fresh contexts showed different Morning Commute checkpoint answer orders for Inference.
manualQa.progress: Screenshots opened Morning Commute cards in Preview mode and did not press Continue.

knownIssues: Vectors and Tensors are readable but still too abstract for the target audience without stronger interactions.
Prompt vs Response demo is tall on mobile; bottom navigation can cover the lower part of the demo until the learner scrolls.
The review prompt referenced src/data/contentReview.ts, but the current repo file is src/data/contentReview.js.
nextImplementationPrompt: Implement a v0.19.1 Morning Commute visual/interaction repair pass without adding cards, games, heavy 3D libraries, generated PNGs, or progress/badge/randomization changes.
